chunks.text: Order Sheet IN THE HIGH COURT OF SINDH, BENCH AT SUKKUR C. P No. D – 1767of 2018 Date Order with Signature of Hon’ble Judge Hearing of case For hearing of main case 01.11.2022 None present for the petitioner Mr. Muhammad Aslam Jatoi, Assistant Attorney General along with Munir Ahmed, Assistant Superintendent, Post Office Khairpur <><><><>..<><><><> Through instant petition, the petitioner seeks his appointment on the deceased quota in Postal Services, Government of Pakistan, on the ground that his father, namely, Muhammad Din served as Post Man in the said department and died on 22.08.1997 during his services. In response, joint comments have been filed by the respondents, wherein it has stated that father of the petitioner was Federal Government Employee and the cases of such employees are governed under the Civil Servant Act, 1973. Likewise, the rules applicable to them and cases relating to them are dealt through Appointment, Promotion and Transfer (APT) Rules, 1973 and it does not contain any provision as of Rule 11-A of the Sindh Civil Servants (Appointment, Promotion and Transfer) Rules, 1973 as referred to by the petitioner in Para 6 of the petition, which is not applicable to the cases of Federal Government Employees ipso-facto. The petitioner has failed to rebut the aforesaid comments of the respondents by filing any counter-affidavit or objections. However, it is a legal position that Rule 11-A (ibid) is not applicable in the cases of employees of Federal Government, under that the children / spouse of the deceased employee of Sindh Government can be appointed under deceased quota. Hence, this petition being devoid of any merits is dismissed accordingly.

However, it is a legal position that Rule 11-A (ibid) is not applicable in the cases of employees of Federal Government, under that the children / spouse of the deceased employee of Sindh Government can be appointed under deceased quota. Hence, this petition being devoid of any merits is dismissed accordingly. Judge Judge ARBROHI
